text: Warehouse pedestrian struck by a forklift/PIT when operator did not look in direction of travel; foot crush led to surgical toe amputations. Multi-employer warehouse environment; incident reporting requirements not met.
Crane mechanic inspecting trolley wheels during movement was crushed between a moving bumper and a fixed guard; lack of spotter and poor visibility/communication contributed to fatality.
Worker grinding and welding galvanized gate without respirator experienced hot metal burn to ear and illness consistent with fume exposure; inadequate PPE and incomplete safety program noted.
Painter used a stepladder as a single ladder on sloped tiled roof; ladder slipped causing ~19 ft fall with head laceration. Unsafe ladder practice was reported as common on site.
Utility worker setting up roadside work zone was struck by passing motorist within minutes of arrival before controls were fully established; fatality.

During drilling rig disassembly, releasing pin removed while worker was positioned on the leg; falling component pinned worker causing fatal crush injury. Training on correct pin sequence and exclusion zone were inadequate.
Worker contacted unguarded generator fan blades while retrieving equipment in trailer power center; fatal laceration injuries. Guarding and isolation controls were lacking.
Worker adjusting belts on machine was injured when coworker started equipment; sleeve caught in sprocket pulling hand in. Scene changed before investigation; no lockout/tagout program.

Weathertightness risk: monolithic cladding and joint detailing may trap moisture; inspect for cracks and confirm drainage/ventilation strategy.
Regulatory/QA risk: incomplete consent and inspection records can allow latent defects (fire or moisture) to persist into operation and trigger expensive remediation.
